name: Ensure that Elasticsearch database is not exposed to the internet (ports 9200 and/or 9300)
body:
### Security intent

This control reduces unnecessary exposure by ensuring that **that Elasticsearch database is not exposed to the internet (ports 9200 and/or 9300)**.

### Network condition reviewed

The assessment reviews elasticsearch database that are exposed to the internet (ports 9200 and/or 9300) in AWS. It focuses on rules, endpoints, listeners, or policies that make the service reachable in a riskier way than intended.

### Expected posture

Public reachability should be deliberate, documented, and tightly scoped. Administrative and data-plane paths should use private connectivity, strong TLS, explicit source ranges, and provider-native controls rather than broad internet access.
description: Elasticsearch database that are exposed to the internet (ports 9200 and/or 9300)
remediationDescription:
## AWS remediation

Restrict Elasticsearch/OpenSearch ports 9200 and 9300 to trusted sources only.

### Console
1. Open affected security group.
2. Remove 0.0.0.0/0 and ::/0 ingress on ports 9200 and 9300.
3. Allow only trusted CIDRs or app security groups.

### AWS CLI
~~~bash
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id <sg-id> \
  --ip-permissions IpProtocol=tcp,FromPort=9200,ToPort=9200,IpRanges='[{CidrIp=0.0.0.0/0}]'
aws ec2 revoke-security-group-ingress \
  --group-id <sg-id> \
  --ip-permissions IpProtocol=tcp,FromPort=9300,ToPort=9300,IpRanges='[{CidrIp=0.0.0.0/0}]'
~~~

### References
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-security-groups.html
- https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/ec2/revoke-security-group-ingress.html
